content: codex-claude-bridge documents and supports exactly two models: `gpt-5.5` as the default (current flagship) and `gpt-5.4` as the documented fallback for when the ChatGPT-subscription tier of Codex doesn't yet have the current flagship. Older Codex variants (`gpt-5.3-codex`, `gpt-5.2-codex`, `gpt-5.1-codex-mini`) are NOT listed in the README model table and should not be recommended in error messages, tips, or docs. Do not re-propose mini models as smaller/faster options — the project position is flagship-only with a one-generation fallback.
context: User directive on 2026-04-23 after the v0.3.3 release cycle: 'we do 5.5 if not 5.4' and 'we dont need mini models'. The README previously listed five variants (gpt-5.5, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.3-codex, gpt-5.2-codex, gpt-5.1-codex-mini). Pruned in commit d9da0c2 to just 5.5 and 5.4. Error-message tips and classifier hints already only mention those two, so no code change was needed beyond the README. When future flagships ship, the pattern continues: new flagship becomes default, old flagship becomes documented fallback, the previous fallback drops from the table.
title: Supported models: gpt-5.5 default, gpt-5.4 fallback. No mini variants.
